game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Strange Horticulture:** Strange Horticulture is an occult puzzle game where players manage a plant shop, primarily interacting with a desk-based UI via taps and drags. Each in-game day, customers arrive with requests for specific plants, providing cryptic clues or symptoms, which players must deduce by cross-referencing a growing inventory of 50+ unique plants with an in-game encyclopedia containing detailed descriptions and illustrations. Players drag and drop the identified plant from their shelves onto the customer's order slot, and incorrect identifications may lead to adverse story consequences or customer dissatisfaction. Further progression involves using textual clues from customers or found notes to click on specific locations on a regional map, revealing new plants or lore, with successful identification unlocking new encyclopedia entries and advancing the overarching narrative mystery. The game's difficulty scales as clues become more abstract and the variety of similar-looking plants increases, requiring more precise observation and deduction to avoid story-altering mistakes.
game_info.description: Welcome to Botanical Mysteries! You run a mysterious plant shop where customers arrive with cryptic clues about plants they need. Study your encyclopedia of rare plants, match customer descriptions to the correct specimens, and fulfill orders to progress through increasingly challenging days. Incorrect identifications may upset customers and affect your reputation. Master the art of botanical deduction to unlock all plant varieties and solve the occult mystery hidden within your shop.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between encyclopedia pages, plant inventory, and customers
Space: Select/confirm plant for customer order
Shift: Toggle between encyclopedia and inventory view
Z: Cancel current selection
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic game mechanics including navigation through UI elements, encyclopedia viewing, and simple plant selection. Validates that the player can interact with all major game systems without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through encyclopedia pages, browse inventory, and attempt to fulfill simple customer orders with obvious clues. Test UI transitions and basic interaction flows.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully navigate all UI elements, view encyclopedia entries, select plants from inventory, and complete at least one customer order without crashes. Game state updates correctly after actions.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests optimal gameplay strategy to win the game by correctly identifying all plants for customers across multiple days. Validates win condition achievement through perfect deduction and matching.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze customer clues, cross-reference with encyclopedia descriptions systematically, and select the correct plant for each customer. Progress through all days by maintaining perfect accuracy and building reputation.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Complete all customer orders correctly, achieve maximum reputation, progress through all game days, and reach GAME_OVER_WIN state with a perfect score.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests failure conditions by deliberately selecting incorrect plants to validate game over mechanics and customer dissatisfaction system.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally provide wrong plants to customers, accumulate reputation penalties, and trigger the lose condition.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Game correctly detects incorrect plant selections, reduces reputation, and triggers GAME_OVER_LOSE when reputation reaches zero.
